title: 中考六选五 19: When Confidence Becomes Carelessness
passage:
Confidence is important for students. It helps them speak up, try difficult tasks and continue after failure. 1. ______ When confidence becomes too strong, however, it may turn into carelessness.

One sign of overconfidence is skipping basic steps. A student may think a maths problem is easy and stop checking the question carefully. 2. ______ Many mistakes happen not because the student cannot solve the problem, but because he or she moves too fast.

Another sign is refusing feedback. Confident students may believe their first answer must be right. 3. ______ If they cannot listen to correction, their progress will slow down.

True confidence is different from thinking you are always right. It allows students to try bravely, but also to check honestly. 4. ______ A truly confident learner is not afraid to find and fix mistakes.

Students should keep a balance. They should believe in their ability, but they should also respect the difficulty of each task. 5. ______ This balance can help them perform better in exams and in life.

questions.label: Questions 1-5 - Sentence Insertion
questions.instruction: Choose the best sentence from A-F for each blank in the passage. There is one extra sentence you do not need.

choices.text: As a result, they may miss important details.
But confidence does not mean ignoring possible problems.
They may treat advice as unnecessary criticism.
Careful confidence is stronger than blind confidence.
It is one of the qualities that supports successful learning.
Students should avoid confidence because it usually leads to mistakes.
